creatures.title: Abomination
creatures.content:
Abomination is a species encountered in wandering groups in Path of the Damned, Titan's Wound, and Forgotten Lab.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Cloth Abomination 		Death Extra Padding: At the start of battle, this creature gains a Barrier that absorbs damage equal to 200% of its highest stat. 42 22 12 17 17 Obtain Abomination Plush and bother it repeatedly 1

Ritual Abomination 		Death Turn to Grey: After this creature is Attacked, enemies are afflicted with Vulnerable. 42 22 12 17 17 Path of the Damned, Titan's Wound, Forgotten Lab 10

Abomination Brute 		Death Flesh Rot: After an enemy is afflicted with either Weak or Vulnerable, this creature afflicts them with whichever of these debuffs the target doesn't already have. 42 22 12 17 17 Path of the Damned, Titan's Wound, Forgotten Lab 15

Flesh Abomination 		Death Wretchedness: Your creatures' attacks against enemy creatures with Vulnerable cannot be Dodged and have a 50% greater chance to deal critical damage. This trait does not stack. 36 23 13 18 18 Path of the Damned, Titan's Wound, Forgotten Lab 30

Abomination Volatile 		Death Quell: When this creature Attacks an enemy with Vulnerable, it deals 100% more damage. If this damage exceeds 35% of the target's Current Health, the target dies. 40 23 13 18 18 Path of the Damned, Titan's Wound, Forgotten Lab 45

Gore Abomination 		Death Manipulate Fear: Enemy creatures with Weak have 50% less Attack and cannot deal critical damage. This trait does not stack. 36 23 13 18 18 Path of the Damned, Titan's Wound, Forgotten Lab 60

Wandering Abomination 		Death Sin and Sentence: After this creature Attacks an enemy with both Weak and Vulnerable, it has a 50% chance to kill the target. 39 22 12 17 17 Path of the Damned, Titan's Wound, Forgotten Lab 75

Bile Abomination 		Death Frailty: When this creature Attacks an enemy with Vulnerable, it deals 100% more damage, recovers Health equal to 100% of the damage dealt, and removes this debuff. 42 22 12 17 17 Path of the Damned, Titan's Wound, Forgotten Lab 90

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Abomination: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Abominations, enemies always have Weak and Vulnerable, and take 50% more damage. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

On a Master of Abominations team, Ritual Abomination and Abomination Brute are made redundant as there is no longer a need to apply Weak and/or Vulnerable. While the  Defiler specialization has traits that enhance Weak and Vulnerable, the funnier strategy is to use  Trickster's Even the Odds, a start-of-battle instant-kill effect, to cause Wandering Abominations with Centaur Raider's Anticipation trait to fish for a turn-zero victory.

As of version 2.0, when an enemy in a boss fight is hit with an instant-kill effect, they will instead take damage equal to 20% of their Missing Health.

Trivia[edit]

This species is most likely based on Frankenstein's Monster, an undead sewn together from body parts of other people.

Apis Majesty 		Life Necessitarianism: At the start of battle, this creature gains 100% Attack, Intelligence, Defense, and Speed. This creature deals 70% less damage. 32 22 12 19 19 The Barrens, Overgrown Temple 60

Apis Protector 		Life Righteous Concession: This creature and the creatures adjacent to it are resistant to debuffs. 31 21 11 21 21 The Barrens, Overgrown Temple 75

Apis Warrior 		Life Crunch: This creature's attacks deal 75% more damage and afflict the target with Vulnerable. 27 22 12 22 22 The Barrens, Overgrown Temple 90

Apis Adolescent 		Life Primed And Ready: After an enemy Casts a spell on all your creatures, this creature Attacks it for 300% normal damage. 26 21 11 21 21 Nature Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Apis: 

At the start of battle, your  Apises gain the Long Live trait.

Strategy[edit]

Apis Defender is an awesome creature: normally ensuring a creature would intercept attacks would require acting first and Provoking, but Defender does it for free!

Also Apis Protector is an awesomely versatile creature: oneself and adjecent allies gain debuff resistance without Any Conditions, so your creatures get enough stabiliy agsinst some strongly restrictive defuffs; Frozen, Silenced, Snared, and more.

The other Apises have high potentialities as Attacker or Tunk, But let's be careful that some Apises are stats-changing or decresing dealing damages with their own traits.

Trivia[edit]

Apis is a deified bull in ancient Egyptian mythology.

Your  Apocalypses act as if they take 2 turns at once. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Quite obvious synergy with Reaver. Upon making it to Gate of the Gods, Anneltha or Lister will speed the snowball along.

Trivia[edit]

"Apocalypse" means "revelation" in ancient greek; originally (in 6th century BC, in jewish Babylonian exile) apocalypticism was mysticism based on visions (rather than god talking to people), but due to many of them being about the end of the world, word "apocalypse" currently means "end of the world".

creatures.title: Arachnalisk
creatures.content:
Arachnalisks are a race encountered by interacting with Spider Eggs in the Arachnid Nest. Arachnalisks work to prevent enemies from taking a turn until their Poisoned debuff is strong enough to kill them.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Canopy Arachnalisk 		Nature Here to Die: After an enemy is afflicted with either Bleeding or Poisoned, this creature afflicts them with whichever of these debuffs the target doesn't already have. 22 23 23 13 18 Arachnid Nest (Eggs) 34

Dragonlair Arachnalisk 		Nature Numbing Venom: After an enemy takes damage from Poisoned, they have a 50% chance to be afflicted with Snared. This trait does not stack. 28 23 23 13 18 Arachnid Nest (Eggs) 34

Spitting Arachnalisk 		Nature Spit Poison: After this creature is Attacked, it afflicts enemies with Poisoned. If enemies already have this debuff, its potency is increased by 30% instead. 28 23 23 13 18 Arachnid Nest (Eggs) 34

Goliath Arachnalisk 		Nature Entrapment: After this creature is Attacked, it afflicts the enemy with Snared. 28 23 23 13 18 Arachnid Nest (Eggs) 45

Woolly Arachnalisk 		Nature Arachnophobia: Enemies with Snared take 100% more damage from their Poisoned debuffs. When they break free, they take damage equal to 5 turns of their Poisoned debuffs. This trait does not stack. 28 23 23 13 18 Arachnid Nest (Eggs) 60

Ancient Arachnalisk 		Nature Consume: When this creature Attacks an enemy with Snared, it deals 150% more damage, recovers Health equal to 100% of the damage dealt, and removes this debuff. 28 23 23 13 18 Arachnid Nest (Eggs) 75

Creeping Arachnalisk 		Nature Hopelessness: Enemies with Snared take 50% more damage. This trait does not stack. 28 23 23 13 18 Arachnid Nest (Eggs) 90

Razorweb Arachnalisk 		Nature Jagged Edges: At the start of each creature's turn, enemies with Snared take damage equal to 10% of this creature's Speed. This trait does not stack. 36 16 16 16 26 Nature Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Arachnalisk: 

Enemies take 20% more damage from Poisoned for each  Arachnalisk fighting on your side. This trait does not stack.

Note: According to The Great Big List of Siralim Ultimate Formulas and Errata, Master of Arachnalisks "literally does NOTHING," but (as of 10/29/25) it hasn't yet been updated with changes from version 2.0 forward.

Strategy[edit]

Arachnalisks pretend that their niche is to drive a  Sorcerer team forward using a Poisoned-Snared debuff combo. Their actual niche is disappointing the player, and it goes further than their defective Mastery trait. When Dragonlair Arachnalisk's Numbing Venom applies Snared, the Snared creature does not get stuck at the bottom of the timeline until it finishes its turn. Spitting Arachnalisk and Goliath Arachnalisk asking to be Attacked while having base 13 Defense is an unfunny joke; they are at real risk of self-destruction when they attempt to use their traits, even when they cast Sabotage on their own teammates.

Outside their niche, the spiders aren't all that bad. A  Hell Knight using Here to Die and Flamehook Pilwiz's Kindling can apply Poisoned to all six enemies on turn zero, giving them a second source of Attack-based indirect damage. Their below-average Defense increases the damage dealt by the  Toxicologist Specialization's Prognosis perk, and Woolly Arachnalisk's new-and-improved Arachnophobia adds an additional way to proc Poison damage on an enemy.

creatures.title: Avatar
creatures.content:
Gate of the Gods content

This page or section contains information about things that will be encountered at Realm Depth 240 and deeper. Spoiler alert!

 Avatars are Gods of Rodia incarnated as creatures. You can obtain their mana by challenging them in the Gate of the Gods, and winning.

After you defeat a god, you will obtain some of its Mana (10% per difficulty, so 1 win at difficulty 10 will give 100% - enough to summon). After you acquire 100% Mana, you can summon that god's Avatar to join your party just like a normal creature. You can also use your Mana to upgrade your existing Avatar creatures at the Godforge (you will also need 100 Emblems to do that, though).

Each Avatar has a powerful trait along with an exclusive Ultimate Spell. Ultimate Spells are extremely powerful spells that have only 1 Charge, but this Charge regenerates after each battle (because they are ethereal).

You can only have 1 Avatar in your party at a time (unless you're Fanatic who can have up to 3).


Creature Class Trait Health Attack Intelligence Defense Speed Ultimate Spell
4080 Chaos Robotic Armor: Your creatures are resistant to debuffs. After your creatures are afflicted with a debuff, they have a 50% chance to gain Immune. This trait does not stack. 38 18 18 18 18 Heat Beam: Target is killed. If it cannot be killed, it takes a devastating amount of damage 3 times. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.

This spell is an Ultimate Spell.

Avatars have no Mastery trait.

Avatars can be fused, but cannot be the secondary fusion.

Avatars can be upgraded at the Godforge for permanent boosts, such as more base stats, strength against classes, etc.

Strategy[edit]

Avatars provide extremely powerful traits to your team. For some examples: Genaros will let Bloodmage scale ridiculously; Torun will allow Paladin to do more actions per turn; Surathli is universally great for tankiness of your team; Regalis opens a whole new can of funny builds; Anneltha powers up Reaver greatly.

With Godforge bonuses, Avatars can be used to supplement traits/perks that require high base stats.

Anointing Multitheism will allow you to benefit from Avatar traits using  Godspawn.

Anointing Army of Gods will allow you to have up to three Avatars on your team at once.

Trivia[edit]

Word "Avatar" comes from a Hindu word for a concept of divine descent of a deity from their divine abode to the world; a related word, "avatarana" is a term for an actor moving from stage wings to the stage.

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Basilisk: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Basilisks, they have a 20% chance to avoid damage. In addition, their innate traits have a 25% chance to activate even if they took damage between their turns. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

A creature's Basilisk trait(s) activate when it is sent to the top of the timeline, regardless of if it's able to take action before being displaced by another creature being sent to the top of the timeline. When a creature is sent to the top of the timeline, its turn counter raises, regardless of if it's able to take action before being displaced by another creature being sent to the top of the timeline, perhaps triggering its Basilisk trait in the process, perhaps while benefitting from  Apocalypse traits or  Reaver perks. The Timewalk spell is a good way to trigger Basilisk traits artificially.

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Cataclysm: 

If all of the creatures in your party are  Cataclysms, their traits have a 10% chance to activate at the start of each creature's turn as well. This trait does not stack.

Lore[edit]

Cataclysms are highly volatile, highly illegal weapons created by condensing several Spell Gems into an "eye" over the course of days. They unleash their spell uncontrollably, posing a constant danger to everyone nearby. While it is possible to safely contain a cataclysm, its spell-casting creates a buildup of pressure which will eventually burst its container if ignored. Sealed cataclysms are sometimes used as bombs.

Strategy[edit]

Chimera traits with stats tied to classes affect the following:

  • Life: Corresponds to the Health (HP) stat.
  • Death: Corresponds to the Defense stat.
  • Chaos: Corresponds to the Attack stat.
  • Sorcery: Corresponds to the Intelligence stat.
  • Nature: Corresponds to the Speed stat.

creatures.title: Dryad
creatures.content:
Dryads are a race exclusive to the God Shop of Shallan in the Fae Lands.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Dryad Naturalist 		Nature Extension: After this creature Defends, the duration of your creatures' buffs is extended by 2 turns, and your creatures' minions gain 1 stack. 28 16 16 21 21 Shallan God Shop (Favor Rank 5) 64

Dryad Proliferator 		Nature Moonlight: Your creatures have 7% more Attack, Intelligence, and Defense for each buff they have. This trait does not stack. 29 17 17 22 22 Shallan God Shop (Favor Rank 8) 64

Dryad Raincaller 		Nature Soothing Rain: After this creature Defends, your creatures recover 20% Health for each buff they have. 32 17 17 21 21 Shallan God Shop (Favor Rank 11) 64

Dryad Seductress 		Nature Green Eyes: After any creature gains a buff, this creature gains that buff as well. 31 16 16 21 21 Shallan God Shop (Favor Rank 14) 64

Dryad Warden 		Nature Duplication: After this creature Provokes, your other creatures gain a copy of this creatures' buffs. 30 15 15 23 20 Shallan God Shop (Favor Rank 18) 64

Dryad Vindicator 		Nature Absolution: After an enemy is afflicted with a debuff, they lose 15% Attack and Intelligence. This trait does not stack. 31 16 16 21 21 Shallan God Shop (Favor Rank 25) 64

Dryad Sunbather 		Nature Oulala: Enemies that are Confused deal 150% more damage to each other with attacks. This trait does not stack. 28 16 16 21 21 Nature Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Dryad: 

Your  Dryads' buffs and minions last forever.

Strategy[edit]

While Dryads may be typecast in  Warden teams, sometimes  Cleric ones as well, the myriad of ways to apply Buffs and Debuffs allows nearly any build to benefit from at least one Dryad trait. Dryad Vindicator and Dolor Sin create a Stat Decrease-Debuff loop that, with the right statblock, can deal massive amounts of indirect damage via  Defiler's Unholy Night perk, or quickly neuter an enemy when combined with Leper Plaguespreader's innate trait. Once a  Necromancer gets access to Andrick's Tattered Garb during the postgame, Dryad Keeper's Surge can cause each of their creatures' offensive stats to skyrocket with few or no changes in strategy. Even when it isn't the main focus of a build, any Specialization that can rapidly apply Buffs and Debuffs, such as  Witch Doctor or  Brewmaster, can revel in Dryad traits, because having Buffs and having Stats are just plain good. The only limit to teambuilding with Dryads is how much time you're willing to spend in the Fae Lands.

Amphisbaena Vaccinator will help proc Absolution and loses its trait's downside with Defiler's perk Lingering Sickness (which became Annointable in 2.0!), while Amphisbaena Prospector will help proc Surge and loses its trait's downside with Master of Dryads. Pair the innate traits of Dryad Seductress and Ectoplasmic Slime for a great passive Buffing support.

creatures.content:
Electropods are a race encountered in the Forgotten Lab and the Gambler's Hive in wandering groups. Like Vin Diesel, these electronic arthropods have a need for Speed and know the value of family.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Electropod Mantis 		Nature Networking: Your creatures' Electropod traits act as if your party contains 3 more Electropod creatures. This trait does not stack. 30 15 15 20 25 Forgotten Lab, Gambler's Hive 64

Electropod Ant 		Nature Thread Pool: Your Electropod creatures have 10% more Speed for each Electropod fighting on your side. This trait does not stack. 28 16 16 21 26 Forgotten Lab, Gambler's Hive 64

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Electropod: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Electropod, they gain 50% more Speed from stat-boosting effects. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Electropods' Speed addiction gives them obvious synergy with the  Windrunner specialization, but their various traits promoting race-stacking makes them an excellent choice for  Tribalists as well. Whether gaining Speed on a Windrunner team or having Speed on a Tribalist team, using Electropods leads to the same end result: dealing game-breaking damage using Lead the Charge.

For a Tribalist team, double-down on the Specialization's horde mentality using  Cockatrice traits, Sphinx Elder's Pharaoh's Boon, Sphinx Ordainer's Blessing of Sands, and, eventually, Shackler's Signet's two imaginary Electropods. By pairing the Tribalist perk We Are Pack with Clawing Cockatrice's Outcast No Longer, it only takes one Electropod to run a Master of Electropods team. This is fantastic, since the traits of Draco Chimera and Underdweller Mischiefmaker incentivize using Electropods as the secondary fusions because the Nature class is associated with Speed.

Trivia[edit]

One of the species contibuted by an unknown backer.

After your creatures are healed by Mending, a random enemy is healed for that amount as well. This trait does not stack. 35 20 25 20 10 Cutthroat Jungle 1

Your creatures with Sleeping have a 50% chance to afflict the target with Sleeping with attacks and spells. This trait does not stack. 32 25 20 15 18 Cutthroat Jungle 1

Triflaratops 		Nature Molten Spray: This creature always has Scorned. After your creatures with Scorned Attack, they deal damage to each enemy equal to 50% of the damage dealt. This trait does not stack. 35 25 15 20 15 Cutthroat Jungle 1

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Elementasaur: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Elementasaurs, they are resistant to debuffs and at the start of battle, they share the debuffs referenced in their traits with each other. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Traits like Favored Masochist's Lust for Punishment, Masochist Angler's Under Stress, and Spirits' Numbed Pain were created for a time when "Always Have" debuffs weren't inflicted on the traitholder's side. On a Master of Elementasaurs team, having all three of the listed traits at the start of battle immediately grants 240% more sourced damage output, six dodge and critical damage rerolls, and a 30% chance to negate damage received.

While the above may sound appealing, using even one Elementasaur on a team expects you to remake your strategy in its image, while using six is a juggling act. Triflaratops and Thunder-Rex's traits have mutually-exclusive benefits and require your creatures to resist debuffs. Windactyl's Sandstorm, without any Shapeshifter traits active, is a recipe for friendly-fire - however, on a Master of Elementasaurs team, friendly-fire may be desirable, as Holyguanodon's Ignorance is Bliss requires opportunities to heal. Vinosaurus' Barbed Vines fares well enough supporting a non-Elememtasaur team, namely among  Manticores, but it only belongs on an Elementasaur team if you're seriously confident in your creatures' ability to proc Casts and/or Attacks.

Trivia[edit]

The  Elementasaur race was contributed by the backer "Playmikesux".

In addition, each creature of the species takes a real-world dinosaur for inspiration: Tyrannosaurus Rex, Triceratops, Velociraptor, Spinosaurus, Brachiosaurus, Pterodactyl, Ankylosaurus, Deinonychus, and Iguanodon, respectively.

creatures.title: Exotic
creatures.content:
Exotics are a "species" of unique creatures encountered in Otherworld Portals. They don't belong to any other species, and are therefore exotic.

Contents

  • 1 Otherworld Portals
  • 2 How do I get more Exotics?
  • 3 List
  • 4 Trivia

Otherworld Portals[edit]

Otherworld Portals are very rarely encountered in Realms. They look like rainbow gates that lead to a boss room, in which a creature group led by the  Exotic creature resides.

How do I get more Exotics?[edit]

In 2.0, you can buy all exotic creatures from the Nethermancer for 10,000 stardust. You acquire stardust by defeating exotics.

Otherwise, to get more Mana of an Exotic you already have, you will need Knowledge of them. You can do this in two ways.

  1. The first way is by accumulating Knowledge through drops, which is unpredictable. This is potentially faster in realms like the Arachnid Nest, Kingdom of Heretics and Overgrown Temple, all of which have increased Knowledge rewards for higher favor.
  2. A more reliable way is to use the Mirrorball and Mysterious orbs in the Azure Dream or Overgrown Temple, making sure you have the Exotic you wish to farm on your team. There is one orb per map, and each time you use it you will fight a copy of your own creatures.

Once you have Knowledge rank D, start a Hunt for the creature you need. The Hunt will elevate them to rank C or higher, and then you can use the Divination Candle to summon and defeat them for summoning.

Trivia[edit]

You can pay 200$ to add your own Exotic to the game. Or 1000$ for a whole new species.

creatures.title: Fae
creatures.content:

Strategy[edit]

Every Godspawn is different, adaptable for different kind of team, but Noxpit is widely considered to be the worst creature in Siralim Ultimate.

Multitheism perk of Fanatic gives Godspawn a trait of their "parent", which is great value since you can sneak 2 avatar traits into your team now without being constrained by Mastery requirement.

Pumking 		Sorcery Pumpkin Spice: At the start of battle, your creatures swap their Maximum Health with their Defense. 40 18 18 18 18 Chaos Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Golem: 

At the start of battle, your  Golems gain the Triple Take trait. If they already have this trait, they gain the Chip Off The Block trait instead.

Mana for Treasure Golem cannot be obtained. When encountering the Treasure Golem, you get mana for Nugget.

Strategy[edit]

Iron Golem's trait is extremely powerful, as it shares already juicy traits of other golems with each other. This won't share traits if you change other creatures' race to  golem (via cockatrice or tribalist) - traits are shared before races are changed. If you can goad enemies into attacking you (with Sabotage or otherwise), Gold Golem's trait will soon make your team impenetrable.

War Golem's trait is awesome both in all-golem team and in fusions - more actions, with no damage reduction, no strings attached!

Maximum Health grows much faster with levels than Defense, so Pumpking can become nearly invulnerable, or very lethal in hands of a Siegemaster.

Trivia[edit]

Golem is a creature from Jewish mysticism and folklore; it is a person made from inanimate matter, usually via use of divine names.

creatures.title: Inquisitor
creatures.content:
This page is about a species. For other uses, see Inquisitor_(specialization).

 Inquisitors are a race encountered in wandering groups in Eternity's End and the Torture Chamber.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Inquisitor Weaver 		Nature Pleasant Disease: When your creatures' Writhelings minions afflict a debuff, they also grant one of your creatures a random buff. In addition, your creatures start battles with 1 Writhelings. This trait does not stack. 35 15 20 22 15 Torture Chamber, Eternity's End 45

Infernal Inquisitor 		Nature Open The Portals: After this creature Attacks, it gains a random minion. After your other creatures Attack, they have a 30% chance to gain a random minion. This trait does not stack. 35 15 20 20 15 Torture Chamber, Eternity's End 60

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Inquisitor: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Inquisitors, they gain a random minion at the start of their turns. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Inquisitors are tailored for a by-the-books  Necromancer build, relying completely on Minions for everything. However, a by-the-books Necromancer is slow by nature; for a faster build, look to a  Hound or  Lich team. Also, Carnivorous Inquisitor becomes obsolete after earning enough Perk Points to max out Midnight Bargain.

On a Master of Inquisitors team, the largest damage output will almost always be indirect damage. Whether raising your creatures' Defense to enhance their Zombies (as the trait The Walking Dead ignores Health), or raising their Health to enhance their Spiderlings, there's one trait your team will feel incomplete without: Synthesis. Have fun hunting for Discarded Blood if you haven't found any already!

Like many other builds, Inquisitors benefit from Dryad Keeper, Dryad Proliferator, and Dryad Vindicator's innate traits. More specifically, Inquisitor Weaver benefits from Writhelings now being able to cause rapid stat gains and losses with minimal effort on your creatures' part. Note that Writhelings have a maximum of 5 stacks, but Nox Sinon's innate trait raises that cap by 3.

creatures.title: Leech
creatures.content:
Leeches are a race encountered in wandering groups in the Arachnid Nest and the Titan's Wound. Unsurprisingly, these creatures are all about enhancing their Leeching buff.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Leech Devourer 		Death Coming Undone: After your creatures with Leeching Attack, they gain 10% Attack, Intelligence, Defense, and Speed. This trait does not stack. 40 23 18 18 13 Arachnid Nest, Titan's Wound 45

Moon Leech 		Death Deeper Sucking: Your creatures' Leeching buff is 100% more effective. This trait does not stack. 42 22 17 17 12 Arachnid Nest, Titan's Wound 75

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Leeche: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Leeches, their Leeching buff lasts forever and heals for 300% more. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

An all-Leech team makes for a weird  Cleric build, a vaguely unsatisfying  Bloodmage build, or a phenomenal  Paladin build. As Leeching works with sourced indirect damage, including the innate traits of Koloss Doombringer, Koloss Champion, and Koloss Eradicator in the build will turn the Retribution perk into an infinite Health scheme! Throw in Preta Cherub's newly-fixed Sundered and Undone trait to double-dip on damage reflection. However, this team does have an Achilles' Heel: unsourced damage from Minions and Debuffs.

creatures.content:
Liches are a race encountered in wandering groups in Path of the Damned and Sanctum Umbra.

Lich Shadowcaster 		Death Sacrimony: Your creatures Cast Death spells as if they have 100% more Intelligence. This trait does not stack. 32 12 27 13 22 Path of the Damned, Sanctum Umbra 15

Lich Hellcaster 		Death Necronomicon: This creature Casts Death spells 2 additional times, but these spells act as if this creature has 50% less Intelligence. 31 12 27 12 22 Path of the Damned, Sanctum Umbra 30

Lich Overseer 		Death Dark Aegis: Your creatures have 7% more Defense for each non-Ethereal Death Spell Gem each of your creatures have equipped. Maximum of 3 Spell Gems per creature. This trait does not stack. 28 13 28 13 23 Path of the Damned, Sanctum Umbra 45

Lich Bloodletter 		Death Heretic Blood: Your creatures' Death spells now cost 20% Health and don't consume any Charges. 31 12 27 12 22 Path of the Damned, Sanctum Umbra 60

Lich King 		Death Hail to the King: At the start of battle, this creature gains Summon Conquest, Summon War, Summon Death, and Summon Famine. 31 13 28 13 23 Path of the Damned, Sanctum Umbra 75

Lich Necromancer 		Death Necromastery: Your creatures' Spell Gems that summon minions do not consume any Charges. 31 12 27 12 22 Path of the Damned, Sanctum Umbra 90

Lich Netherwalker 		Sorcery Watch of the Buried: When this creature gains a minion, that minion's number of stacks is set to the maximum amount. 33 18 18 18 18 Sorcery Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Lich: 

After your  Liches Cast a Death spell, they gain 10% Intelligence. This trait does not stack.

(While Master of Liches is phrased differently than Master of Gargoyles, its effect is similar: when one of the party's Liches casts a Death spell, all of the party's Liches gain 10% Intelligence.)

Strategy[edit]

Trivia[edit]

Liches, from old german for "corpse", are fantasy creatures that are usually really powerful necromancers that achieved immortality.

creatures.title: Lucanian
creatures.content:
Lucanians are a race encountered in wandering groups in The Barrens. These dragons alternate between enhancing their Spells by Attacking and enhancing their Attacks by Casting.

The realm depth at which each  Lucanian first appears is currently unknown, and the values in the table below are a placeholder.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Lucanian Archunter 		Chaos The High Hunt: After this creature Attacks, it gains 10% Intelligence. After this creature Casts, it gains 10% Attack. 35 25 25 15 20 The Barrens 1

Lucanian Aspectal 		Sorcery Consuming Knowledge: After your creatures Attack, their non-Ultimate Spell gems gain 1 Charge. This trait can only activate once per turn for each creature. This trait does not stack. 32 15 28 15 20 The Barrens 1

Lucanian Windrider 		Nature Big Wings: If this creature's Attack and Intelligence are within 100% of each other, it always deals critical damage and enemies cannot dodge its attacks. 35 25 25 15 25 The Barrens 1

Lucanian Soulbinder 		Sorcery Soul Exchange: At the start of battle, your creatures' Attack and Intelligence are set to whichever is highest. 35 25 25 15 20 The Barrens 1

Lucanian Illuminator 		Life The Golden Sign: At the start of battle, your creatures either gain 100% Attack and Scorned, or 100% Intelligence and Silenced. This trait does not stack. 35 28 28 15 14 The Barrens 1

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Lucanian: 

After all start-of-battle effects occur, if all of the creatures in your party are  Lucanians, their Attack and Intelligence are set equal to whichever is highest.

Strategy[edit]

Trivia[edit]

Strategy[edit]

Hell Knight is sure to love Prowling Manticore's trait.

Trivia[edit]

Manticore is a legendary creature from ancient Persian mythology: a lion with a human head and a scorpion's tail. "Manticore" is a greek spelling, while "Martyaxwar" is closer to the persian, meaning "man-eater".

creatures.title: Mimic (species)
creatures.content:
Mimic is a very rare species.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Pandora 		Chaos Pandora's Box: At the start of battle, this creature Casts 5 random spells, one from each class. 22 22 22 22 22 Rare Encounter from Treasure Chests 1

Strategy[edit]

Original Creature Mimic and/or its trait is indispensable, if you can't utilize no other ways to certainly start battles on the top of Timeline.

The Another rarely encountable creature Pandora is very unstable but has great stats and trait can cast 5 random spells like barrage as SoB effect.

Trivia[edit]

Mimics are living treasure chests from Dungeons&Dragons that became popular in other modern fantasy works.

creatures.content:
Rift Dancer is a species encountered in wandering groups in The Barrens, Sanctum Umbra, and Land of Breath & Balance.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Inaer Rift Dancer 		Life Fan Dance: All effects that activate when a creature Defends will activate 1 additional time. This trait does not stack. 31 16 16 16 26 The Barrens, Sanctum Umbra, Land of Breath & Balance 46

Nadin Rift Dancer 		Life Dark Dance: All effects that activate when a creature dies will activate 1 additional time. This trait does not stack. 36 16 16 16 26 The Barrens, Sanctum Umbra, Land of Breath & Balance 46

Ramun Rift Dancer 		Life Sword Dance: All effects that activate when a creature deals critical damage will activate 1 additional time. This trait does not stack. 36 16 16 16 26 The Barrens, Sanctum Umbra, Land of Breath & Balance 60

Apollinaire 		Life Renaissance: All effects that activate when a creature resurrects will activate 1 additional time. This trait does not stack. 31 16 16 16 26 The Barrens, Sanctum Umbra, Land of Breath & Balance 75

Nax Rift Dancer 		Life What Is Left: This creature deals additional damage with attacks and spells equal to 1% of the stats it has gained in the current battle. 33 20 20 15 20 Life Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Rift Dancer: 

If all the creatures in your party belong to the  Rift Dancer race, their innate traits activate 1 additional time. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Rift Dancers are good supportive creatures with high Speed. Nearly any team can find a place for at least one Rift Dancer trait due to their broad utility.

An all-Rift Dancers team using their Mastery trait can perform shockingly effective combo shennanigans, especially as a  Doombringer, the specialization whose entire deal is "combo shennanigans." Their innate traits’ additional activation can bring your enemies to their doom if they can't stop you from dancing.

When an effect deals damage based on a creature's current Health, and the effect is repeated during one proc, the damage dealt will not decrease after each repeat deals damage. Paired with Apollinaire, Troll Youngster's innate trait can kill an enemy the instant it revives. When paired with Aerum Rift Dancer while Master of Rift Dancers is active, Whiptail Clutcher's trait deals damage equal to 90% of the current health of an enemy unfortunate enough to dodge an attack; this can be exploited to its full potential using Marionette Virtuoso's trait.

Note that damage from the traits of Whiptail Clutcher, Troll Youngster and many similar creatures is indirect damage, and is therefore reduced by Defense (albeit at a much lower rate than Attack or Spell damage), and traits such as Phase Knight's Endurance Aura.

creatures.content:
Satyrs are a race encountered by playing flutes found within the Blood Grove.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Satyr Rammer 		Nature Meaningless Mind: Enemies can only gain each stat 1 time per turn. 33 17 22 12 22 Blood Grove (Pipes) 30

Satyr Squire 		Nature Faulty Fortune: Enemies can only gain 1 buff per turn. 32 17 22 11 22 Blood Grove (Pipes) 60

Satyr Guardian 		Nature Scant Stamina: Enemies can only resurrect 1 time per battle. 32 17 22 11 22 Blood Grove (Pipes) 75

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Satyr: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Satyrs, enemies start battles with 20% less stats. This trait does not stack.

Lore[edit]

Satyrs are the descendants of the Blood Grove's first colonists, humans from Vanelin transformed by consuming the Realm's fruit and water. They build settlements in the Grove's treetops, where they experience fewer  Eft attacks, and trade with the underground  Inquisitors through tree trunks hollowed out all the way down to their roots. The first colonists' warlike traditions, tempered by endless Eft predation, has made the Satyrs expert strategists.

Strategy[edit]

Including a Satyr trait or two can help a team cope with the consequences of high Realm Instability. A combo including Satyr Guardian's Scant Stamina and either Corrupted Phoenix's Final Breath or Troll Youngster's Rude Awakening generally speeds up battles, especially ones with many Life creatures, and may even clothesline certain bosses. To further restrict the enemy's other actions, consider the traits of  Tremors,  Vortexes, and  Wyverns.

Trivia[edit]

The Lore entries of Satyr Squire and Satyr Guardian mention a creature named Unleashed Satyr.

Shambler Recruiter 		Death Chronic Affliction: After an enemy takes damage from Blighted, it has a 50% chance to take the same amount of damage again. This trait does not stack. 30 22 22 17 12 Faraway Enclave, Where the Dead Ships Dwell 40

If all the creatures in your party are  Sea Shamblers, they take 12% less damage for each enemy that is afflicted with Blighted. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

The  Shadowbringer specialization appreciates the boosts Sea Shamblers give to damage from Blighted. Shamblers like Shadowbringers just as much, as the perk Touch of Darkness averts the question of where to put healing spells on a Debuff-focused, likely Death-Class team. Sea Shamblers aren't just for Shadowbringers, though. For example, a  Defiler can use Shambler Benefactor's trait to rub salt in foes' wounds.

Trivia[edit]

Globsters are aquatic animals decayed beyond recognition, known for inspiring countless stories of monsters such as sea serpents, modern-day plesiosaurs, and unkillable reptiles.

Yo−Ho! Those guys have higher Attack and Speed with fast breaking traits, so one strong choice for offensive Hell Knight, Windrunner and the other similar builds.

creatures.title: Smith
creatures.content:
Smith is a species purchased from Azural in the Frostbite Caverns. They are the male counterpart to  Gorgons.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Brim Smith 		Sorcery Dissection: When your creatures Attack, they deal additional damage equal to 50% of the total Attack bonus on this creature's artifact. This trait does not stack. 36 21 16 21 16 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 1) 4

Black Crystal Smith 		Sorcery Unbreakable: Your creatures' '[Class] Strength' Artifact properties prevent 50% more damage from that class. This trait does not stack. 33 21 16 21 16 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 11) 4

Depravity Smith 		Sorcery Braze: Your creatures' '[Class] Strength' Artifact properties deal 50% more damage to that class. This trait does not stack. 36 21 16 21 16 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 14) 4

Pestilence Crafter 		Sorcery Incursion: Your creatures' 'Afflict debuff On Damage' properties have a 100% greater chance to activate. This trait does not stack. 37 21 16 21 16 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 18) 4

Hammer Lord 		Sorcery War Forged: Your creatures' 'Gain buff On Damage' properties have a 100% greater chance to activate. This trait does not stack. 34 21 16 21 16 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 25) 4

Disease Crafter 		Sorcery Imbue: Your creatures' Stat Slots provide 15% more benefit. This trait does not stack. 37 22 17 17 17 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 28) 4

Death Crafter 		Sorcery Honed Blades: Your creatures share 15% of their Artifacts' bonuses from Attack, Intelligence, Defense, and Speed with each other. This trait does not stack. 34 19 16 21 16 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 32) 4

War Crafter 		Sorcery Warcraft: This creature's Stat Slots provide 40% more benefit. 35 20 15 20 15 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 35) 4

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Smith: 

At the start of battle, if all the creatures in your party are  Smiths, they share their Artifact's trait with the creature to the left.

Strategy[edit]

  •  Gladiator's Elbow Grease stacks additively with the effects of War Crafter and Disease Crafter, resulting in a total bonus to Artifact stat slots of 90% and 65%, respectively.
  • 'Stat Slots' refers to the stat bonuses an Artifact gives, including the innate stat bonus from the Artifact (e.g. the Intelligence bonus from Staves), but not bonuses from Nether Stones.
  • Death Crafter/Honed Blades:
    • Shares all bonuses to stats an Artifact grants, including innate bonuses, stat slots, and bonuses from Nether Stones.
    • Stacks with  Gladiator's Elbow Grease, Nether Imbuement, and Yseros' The Truth.
  •  Trickster's Whetbone and Prey perks play nicely with the Brim Smith's Dissection trait. Make it even stronger with:
    • Ferro's rank 40 perk will make the bearer's Proficient buff 100% more effective
    • The Mathematics Textbook will boost your Artifact's first stat slot by 400% (so make sure it is Attack)

Strategy[edit]

A Master of Sparktails team with all six fish can succeed regardless of level. With a powerful synergy that turns the enemy's stats against them, even the  Deprived can go far with Sparktails. Most of the team's worries will be the cases where enemies can't instantly kill themselves by damaging your creatures, such as due to the "chance to avoid damage" Realm Property or the traits of certain bosses.

According to The Great Big List, Sparktail Professor's Seeth only boosts damage dealt by other Sparktail traits.

creatures.title: Unicorn
creatures.content:
This species is encountered in Azure Dream by wishing on Stars for glory. Its members are Unicorn Vivifier, Unicorn Consecrator, Pegasus, Unicorn Thundercracker, Unicorn Stormbringer, Unicorn Firewalker, Unicorn Holycaster.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Unicorn Vivifier 		Life Reinvigoration: After this creature Attacks, it resurrects a random ally with 50% Health. 31 21 16 17 22 Azure Dream (Wishing Stars), Tutorial Starter 1

Unicorn Consecrator 		Life Circle of Life: After this creature Attacks, it heals allies for 50% of the damage dealt. 33 23 15 15 20 Azure Dream (Wishing Stars) 25

Unicorn Stormbringer 		Life Righteous Winds: After this creature Attacks, it increases a random ally's Attack and Intelligence by 50%. 30 21 16 16 21 Azure Dream (Wishing Stars) 30

Unicorn Holycaster 		Life Divine Mending: After this creature Attacks, it heals its ally with the lowest Health for 200% of the damage dealt. 32 22 17 14 19 Azure Dream (Wishing Stars) 45

Pegasus 		Life Chrysaor's Ambition: After this creature Attacks, it gives a random ally 2 random buffs. 36 16 16 21 16 Azure Dream (Wishing Stars) 60

Unicorn Thundercracker 		Life Spur of the Heavens: After this creature Attacks, it has a 50% chance to move a random ally to the top of the Timeline. 31 21 17 14 22 Azure Dream (Wishing Stars) 75

Unicorn Firewalker 		Life Eruption: After this creature Attacks, it has a 50% chance to Cast Volcano. 30 21 16 16 21 Azure Dream (Wishing Stars) 90

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Unicorn: 

Your  Unicorns Attack 2 additional times. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Unicorns are a species with high Attack and Speed, have very good on-attack procs, and therefore make for good attackers. Unicorn Vivifier can be used on many team comps for survivability or to easily trigger on-resurrection procs. A good example would be fusing it with Nix Shadowjumper. (expand this one)

Trivia[edit]

Unicorn is a mythical creature from antiquity, found in Indus Valley art and greek works of natural history. Many thought it to be a real animal.

Pegasus is a mythical winged horse-god from Greek mythology, sired by Poseidon.

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Uralos: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Uraloses, they have a 50% chance to avoid indirect damage. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Uralos traits depend on others in their party also being of that species, so you should either use a team of Uraloses or change the species of others via Clawing Cockatrice's trait, Tribalist perk or, worst come to worst, Epiphany spell. Uralos gain stats when they act, and can escalate greatly if a battle isn't finished quickly. I recommend taking traits that interact with stat gain (Doomsday Spectre) and make some actions automatic (for example, fusing Spearmaster with Valkyrie Queen for up to 6 guaranteed triggers of War Dance).

If you have unlocked Gate of the Gods, Avatar of Meraxis will allow Uralos to grow without any limit in prolonged battles.

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Voidlord: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Voidlords, they always have Repelling and take 30% less damage. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Lore[edit]

These creatures can destroy anything they touch, leaving not even atoms. Dragan, a Raven from the Refuge of the Magi, theorizes that those things come from outside the material universe, same as Watchers. After his ambitious experiment to create Laglor was botched, Dragan disappeared and the number of Voidlords started to grow exponentially, and it took efforts of Tenebris and Mortem to create the Bastion of the Void and contain them.

Laughing Wisp 		Life Ethereal: This creature is immune to debuffs and takes 50% less damage. After this creature takes damage, it is sent to the bottom of the Timeline. 36 16 16 16 26 Bastion of the Void (Ghosts) 52

Scheming Wisp 		Life Poof!: This creature always deals critical damage and has an independent 90% chance to Dodge attacks. After this creature takes damage, it is sent to the bottom of the Timeline. 35 15 15 18 25 Bastion of the Void (Ghosts) 52

Wisp Watcher 		Life Spasm: At the start of this creature's turn, it Casts its first five non-Ethereal Spell Gems. After this creature takes damage, it is sent to the bottom of the Timeline. 36 16 16 16 26 Bastion of the Void (Ghosts) 60

Wisp Seer 		Life The Virtue of Patience: At the start of this creature's turn, your creatures gain 3 random buffs. After this creature takes damage, it is sent to the bottom of the Timeline. 37 17 17 15 25 Bastion of the Void (Ghosts) 75

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Wisp: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Wisps, they have a 25% chance to avoid damage and gain Shelled at the start of their turns. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Wisps' traits have both amazing strength and alarming weakness. To work safely, take a little creativities; for some examples, fusing Mimic, cast Timewalk or Wormhole, or use Reaver's Sure-footed perk to negate forcing move.

creatures.title: Wolpertinger
creatures.content:
Wolpertinger is a species encountered in Amalgam Gardens by staring into fire of Neon Braziers.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Docile Wolpertinger 		Nature Ditto: After one of your creatures manually Casts a Scourge spell, this creature Casts the same Scourge spell. 28 18 21 16 21 Amalgam Gardens (Braziers) 64

Vile Wolpertinger 		Nature Fraud: Your creatures' Scourge spells no longer consume Charges and cannot be Sealed. 29 19 19 16 21 Amalgam Gardens (Braziers) 64

Volatile Wolpertinger 		Nature Prey: At the start of battle, your creatures gain a Scourge spell that matches their class. This trait does not stack. 30 20 20 15 20 Amalgam Gardens (Braziers) 64

Viridian Wolpertinger 		Nature Bottom Feeder: Your creatures' Scourge spells also afflict the target with a debuff that is based on the type of Scourge spell that is Cast. This trait does not stack. 27 20 20 15 20 Amalgam Gardens (Braziers) 64

Volcanic Wolpertinger 		Nature Whimsical: Your creatures' Scourge spells deal 100% more damage. This trait does not stack. 28 18 18 16 21 Amalgam Gardens (Braziers) 64

Vampiric Wolpertinger 		Nature Soul Sucker: After your creatures Cast a damaging spell, they recover Health equal to 30% of the damage dealt. This trait does not stack. 29 19 19 16 19 Amalgam Gardens (Braziers) 64

Vicious Wolpertinger 		Nature Colorblind: After this creature Attacks, it Casts a Scourge spell that matches the target's class. 33 23 18 13 18 Amalgam Gardens (Braziers) 64

Vernal Wolpertinger 		Nature Camaraderie: After this creature Defends, it gains a Barrier that absorbs damage equal to 100% of its Maximum Health. If this creature already has a Barrier, it gives it to a random ally instead. 31 21 21 15 20 Nature Guild 415

Villous Wolpertinger 		Nature Echoing Incantation: This creatures' single-target spells have a 35% chance to be Cast 1 additional time. This effect can occur repeatedly. 35 15 25 10 20 Nature Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Wolpertinger: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Wolpertingers, their Scourge spells always deal critical damage and have a 50% chance to be Cast 1 additional time. This effect can occur multiple times in a row. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Trivia[edit]

Wolpertinger is a cryptid from the alpine forests of Bavaria and Baden-Württemberg in Southern Germany.

creatures.title: Wyvern
creatures.content:
Wyvern is a species encountered by interacting with Eggs in Faraway Enclave.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Wyvern Windrider 		Chaos Spell Stealer: After an enemy Casts a spell on all its allies, this creature has a 50% chance to Cast the same spell on your creatures as well. This trait does not stack. 37 16 21 16 21 Faraway Enclave (Eggs) 43

Wyvern Skystalker 		Chaos Deep Inhalation: After an enemy Casts a spell on all your creatures, this creature has a 50% chance to Cast the spell on the enemy creatures as well. This trait does not stack. 35 15 20 15 20 Faraway Enclave (Eggs) 43

Wyvern Sonicscreecher 		Chaos Bounce: After an enemy Casts a single-target spell on one of your creatures, this creature has a 50% chance to Cast the spell on a random enemy. This trait does not stack. 36 16 21 16 21 Faraway Enclave (Eggs) 43

Wyvern Airslayer 		Chaos Wings of Despair: When an enemy would gain a buff, this creature has a 50% chance to gain it instead. 35 15 20 15 20 Faraway Enclave (Eggs) 45

Wyvern Daybreaker 		Chaos Fallen Star: When an enemy would gain stats, this creature has a 50% chance to gain them instead. 33 13 21 16 21 Faraway Enclave (Eggs) 60

Wyvern Toxicfang 		Chaos Currents: After an enemy gains stats, this creature afflicts it with a random debuff. 35 15 20 15 20 Faraway Enclave (Eggs) 75

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Wyvern: 

Your  Wyverns take 80% less damage from spells. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

creatures.title: Yeti
creatures.content:
Yeti is a species encountered by interacting with Frozen Yetis in Frostbite Caverns.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Coldslam Yeti 		Sorcery Ice Nova: After this creature Provokes, each enemy has a 50% chance to be afflicted with Frozen. 34 19 14 22 17 Frostbite Caverns (Frozen Yetis) 4

Chillbreeze Yeti 		Sorcery Thermal Void: Enemies take 50% more damage while they are Frozen. This trait does not stack. 35 20 15 20 15 Frostbite Caverns (Frozen Yetis) 15

Icemane Yeti 		Sorcery Lingering Frost: After an enemy's Frozen debuff expires, this creature afflicts it with Snared. 36 21 16 21 16 Frostbite Caverns (Frozen Yetis) 30

Frostbite Yeti 		Sorcery Brain Freeze: After this creature is Attacked, the enemy is afflicted with Frozen. 33 18 16 21 16 Frostbite Caverns (Frozen Yetis) 45

Snowsting Yeti 		Sorcery Cold Snap: When this creature damages a Frozen enemy, it deals 300% more damage and removes the debuff. 36 21 16 21 16 Frostbite Caverns (Frozen Yetis) 60

Rimefist Yeti 		Sorcery Frost Breath: After this creature Dodges or deals critical damage, each enemy has a 50% chance to be afflicted with Frozen. 33 18 16 21 16 Frostbite Caverns (Frozen Yetis) 75

Ancient Yeti 		Sorcery Winter Has Come: At the start of battle, this creature afflicts 2 random enemies with Frozen. 36 21 16 21 16 Frostbite Caverns (Frozen Yetis) 90

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Yeti: 

At the start of battle, enemies are afflicted with Frozen if all the creatures in your party are  Yetis.

Strategy[edit]

overview.description: Artifacts grant your creatures bonuses. Each creature can equip one Artifact. You can obtain Artifacts by commissioning them from Sabon the Blacksmith in your castle. Creating, upgrading, and socketing Artifacts costs Brimstone. Artifacts are not obtainable from loot.

stat.note: Stat Health is treated as a base increase; others stack with 'have' effects.

limits.Buff/Debuff triggers: Do not require damage unless target immune

trait.notes: Difficult to find; obtained from Assassination Missions.

spell.note: Spell Gems do not have properties; triggers depend on artifact type.

nether.note: Provides multiple powerful effects; extremely rare. Only Nether Stones survive unsocketing.

mechanics.unsocketing: Removes a property from an artifact; destroys the material (except Nether Stones).
mechanics.branding: Gives an artifact a name for easy identification; no gameplay effect.

 Game description
[
edit

There's a fine line between genius and insanity, and it's difficult to say on what side of that line Animators reside. These fanatical magi spend their days perfecting their Animatus creations: unique creatures that grow more powerful as their master's dark experiments unveil new opportunities.


As an Animator, you will always have a unique creature in your party called an Animatus. Your entire battle strategy will likely focus on this creature. You can empower your Animatus, and even use your other creatures to make it stronger in combat.


(You can, in theory, Not have Animatus on your team. But why would you?)

Relics of the past hold secrets lost to time, and the Antiquarian is determined to uncover them all. This eccentric magi studies the ancient artifacts left behind by the gods themselves, empowering her creatures with forgotten blessings that even the gods have long since forsaken.


As an Antiquarian, you'll specialize in enhancing your creatures' Relics, unlocking hidden abilites that are unique to this specialization. Your creatures' Relics will gain new effects that no other specialization can access, allowing you to customize your team's playstyle in ways that go far beyond the ordinary.

specializations.content:
Astrologer
 is a proc-focused Specialization with a convoluted playstyle. This Spec is the anti-
 
Hell Knight
, micromanaging each creature's Stats and Traits with the end goal of creating the Rodian equivalent of a Rube Goldberg machine.



Contents




1
 
Game Description

Starter creatures

 Game Description
[
edit
]


Astrologers are magi who specialize in creating a sound strategy before each battle. Oftentimes, their plans are so well-constructed that their creatures are able to take combat into their own hands without any further input from their master.


As an Astrologer, you'll plan most of your battle strategies before combat even begins. Astrologers allow their creatures to act on their own under certain conditions.

Strategy
[
edit
]


If you have tunnel vision on 
 
Stars Align
 and its instant-kill effect, an 
 
Astrologer
 build is a race towards the seven required Minions. 
 
Inquisitors
 quickly collect Minions for your creatures, but so can a 
Necro Grimoire
 or 
Mercurial Slime
. 
 
Dryad
 traits capitalize on the Buffs you'll collect, and 
their Mastery trait
 prevents your Dryads' Buffs and Minions from expiring/leaving. Finally, if you plan to use Buffs to gain Minions, have a Plan B for when a 
Nemesis
 enemy or 
Realm Property
 prevents your creatures from gaining Buffs.

Unlocking the 
Gate of the Gods
 at Realm Depth 240 allows you to obtain 
Anneltha
, whose trait keeps several 
 
Astrologer
 perks constantly active.

specializations.content:
Bloodmage
 is a class that is focused on 
Health
 of creatures: with all perks purchased, 
Health

 Game description
[
edit
]


The Blood Mage is a deranged spellcaster who is willing to do anything to overcome his foes - even at the cost of his own creatures' lives. This menacing spellcaster is adept at manipulating the lifeforce of allies and foes alike in his endless pursuit for power.


As a Blood Mage, your creatures will grow more powerful as they take damage. To facilitate these effects, the Blood Mage offers a variety of different ways to inflict damage on his own creatures.

Strategy
[
edit
]


 
Bloodmage
 is one of the easier specs to build for: you will clobber enemies with your healthbar, but otherwise won't have to jump through extra hoops. Look for traits that interact with Maximum and Current 
Health
, such as 
Brownie Brute
's Ultimatum. 
Giant
, 
Koloss
, 
Maniac
 species will serve the bloodmage well. In the late game, 
Avatar of Genaros
 will help 
 
Bloodmage
's team scale to a ridiculous level. 

Especially in the early game, it is easier to stack Maximum Health to take advantage of 
 
Bloodrage
 and 
 
Voracity
 rather than trying to keep your health low to take advantage of the missing Health traits such as 
 
Endless Slaughter
 and 
 
March Towards Death
, which keep you very close to death.

Keep in mind that while 
Health
 scales your damage, you still need 
Speed
 lest enemies just dodge your attacks.

Cabalists can conjure Spell Gems out of thin air to be used by their creatures at any given moment. These magi prefer to employ high-risk, high-reward battle strategies to ensure their victory.


As a Cabalist, you'll enable your creatures to cast spells that they normally wouldn't have access to. While other people's creatures might run out of Spell Gem Charges, you'll be able to keep your creatures well-stocked with fresh Spell Gems at the start of every battle.

Strategy
[
edit
]


The most reliable way of getting ethereal gems you want is to equip normal spellgems and enchant them with Generous. 

 
Djinns
, 
 
Occultists
, 
 
Familiars
 and 
 
Carbuncles
 are all good creatures for a 
 
Cabalist
. Most of the spells your creatures 
Cast
 will be cast automatically, so traits like Superfluidity from 
Lunar Dust
 or The Great Beyond from 
Rhododendron Fae
 will allow you to better take advantage of your perks that require manual spellcasting. 

Dragon Revenant
 and 
Malignant Storm
 will ruin your day- use a 
Delusion Occultist
 or 
Opal Paragon
 to deal with the former. For the latter, make sure your party can deal damage that doesn't come from spells. Otherwise, you can damage the Storm's allies and then cast 
Restore Balance
, or simply have a 
Raven Defiler
 in your party. 
Equipping an anointment to gain 
Defiler
's Wane perk also works. Requires challenging 
False Gods
.


Any spell or trait that causes your creatures to 
Attack
 , 
Defend
, 
Provoke
, or gain Ethereal Gems can be used to multiply your spellcasting potential. Casting 
Elemental Tome
 will cause each of your creatures to cast the spell gems they recieve— 
Mass Taunt
 will create a similar effect, with the added benefit of enabling 
Djinn Evoker
's Mega Bomb. You can even socket these spells into your creatures' artifacts to have them cast automatically!

Note that each creature can cast a maximum of 15 spells a turn, potentially leading to some of your spellcasts being wasted. It's very easy to hit this cap as a 
 
Cabalist
.

The difference between good and evil is often a matter of perspective, but the morality of a Defiler is simple enough for anyone to decide. These dark magi take sadistic pleasure in afflicting their adversaries with crippling diseases, reducing them to weakened husks that no longer have the capacity to defend themselves.


As a Defiler, you'll specialize in debuffs and stat-reducing effects. Enemies will be so weak that your party will be able to easily pick them off, even if they were originally much more powerful than your creatures.

specializations.title: Deprived
specializations.content:
Deprived
 is a Challenge Specialization, which means you pick it if life ain't tough enough for you. If you want to win with this spec, you need to be resourceful indeed with your pick of creatures, artifacts and anointments. 



Contents




1
 
Game description


2
 
Starter creatures

 Game description
[
edit
]


The Deprived was born with nothing and will die with nothing. Cursed with the intelligence of a sponge and the physique of a sickly child, the Deprived must make do with what little fate has left him with.


As a Deprived, you'll lose access to your most significant sources of power, including Fused traits, Avatar creatures, and Relic effects. This is a Challenge Specialization, and it will not appear at the 
Goblet of Giving
 after you unlock it.

specializations.title: Doombringer
specializations.content:
Siralim Ultimate is a game about Shenanigans, and 
 
Doombringer
 allows for shenanigans that are even more bizarre and funny than usual.



Contents




1
 
Game description


2
 
Starter creatures
1


3
 
Perks


4

[
edit
]


The Doombringer draws magic from the Bastion of the Void to fuel his creatures with vast amounts of power. Doombringers specialize in allowing their creatures to perform actions multiple times in a row, all while nullifying his enemies' efforts.


As a Doombringer, your creatures' on-action effects (on-attack, on-cast, etc) can activate additional times, allowing you to chain brutal combos together to devastate your enemies. Doombringers are also able to prevent enemies' on-action effects from occurring as a defensive measure, as well.

You can be up to all sorts of trouble with this class. One example is triggering

specializations.title: Dreamshade
specializations.content:
Dreamshade
 is a mischievous specialization that puts all its chips on a rarely seen debuff - 
Inverted
, usually applied only by things related to 
T'mere M'rgo
. Perks of the class resemble traits of 
Shapeshifters
 that prevent any friendly fire from occuring.

Inverted
 creatures heal enemies instead of damaging them with attacks and spells, and damage allies instead of healing them. This debuff is removed after enough damage or healing has been done to offset its potency.

1
 
Game description


2
 
Starter creatures
1


3

4

[
edit
]


Dreamshades blur the line between dreams and reality. This mystical magi is adept at redirecting his foes' efforts for his own benefit.


As a Dreamshade, you'll utilize the Inverted debuff to cause your foes' damage to instead heal your creatures, and their healing effects to damage their allies.

specializations.title: Druid
specializations.content:
For 
 
Druid
, less is more. To get the most out of this class, you would need to take only 4 creatures, which is 6 traits (counting fused and artifact) less and 2 actions less, but perks of this specialization compensate well for that lack.



Contents




1
 
Game description


2
 
Starter creatures
1


3
 
Perks


4
 
Strategy






 Game description
[
edit
]


Many people foolishly mistake a Druid's introversion for weakness. These magi believe that quality always trumps quantity on the battlefield. Contrary to tradition, they are often seen with fewer than six creatures at their disposal, but the havoc their limited numbers wreak when augmented by Druidic magic is a sight to behold.


As a Druid, you'll probably only want to have one (or up to three, later on) creatures in your party. Your creatures will gain massive benefits for being part of a smaller party.

The

powerful creatures gained from the Gate of the Gods

Shogun
's trait and die due to Kirisute Gomen.

specializations.title: Engineer
specializations.content:
Hey look, buddy. I'm an 
 
Engineer
, that means I solve problems. Whatever ails you, the answer is 
Bomb
. And if that don't work, use more 
Bomb
.

Like 
 
Pyromancer
 and 
 
Toxicologist
, 
 
Engineer
 puts all chips on a damage-over-time effect, except 
Bomb
 is a lot of damage, over one turn. Three turns with upgrades. Also, 
Microbots
 - a minion that deals 50% of its master's 
Intelligence
 per stack at the end of the turn to a random enemy (designed by me, built by me, and you best hope not comin' for you).

specializations.title: Evoker
specializations.content:
Evoker
 is 
the
 spellcasting class. 
 
Evoker
 loves casting spells, and doesn't let anything stand in the way of that love.

Evokers are the ultimate spellcasters. They excel at casting enhanced versions of spells multiple times in a row to overwhelm their enemies before they can act.


As an Evoker, you'll empower your creatures' spellcasting capabilities. Your creatures will be able to cast the same spell multiple times in one turn, and these spells will be much stronger than if they had cast them without your guidance.

specializations.title: Fanatic
specializations.content:
Gate of the Gods content
This page or section contains information about things that will be encountered at 
Realm Depth 240
 and deeper. Spoiler alert!


 
Fanatic
 is a powerful specialization focused around Rodian 
Gods
' 
 
Avatars
 and their 
 
Godspawn
. 
 
Avatars
 are mighty creatures, so taking three instead of one makes for a fearsome band indeed; and 
 
Multitheism
 granting 
 
Godspawn
 traits of their makers means you'll have access to SIX extremely powerful traits.



Contents




1
 
Game description


2
 
Requirements


3
 
Starter creatures
1


4
 
Perks


5
 
Strategy






 Game description
[
edit
]


Fanatics devote their lives to serving the gods. From dusk until dawn, these magi toil away at a neverending list of demands to better serve each of the 30 gods of Rodia. Such devotion is not without purpose, however, for the Fanatic is able to call on the gods' power to serve him in his time of need.


As a Fanatic, you'll be able to take additional 
 
Avatar
 creatures with you on your journey and empower them and their 
Ultimate Spell Gems with potent magic. Fanatics also make use of 
 
Godspawn
 creatures and create powerful synergies between them and their 
 
Avatar
 allies.

[
edit
]


To begin the 
project

Avatar
 creature in your party when you speak to Everett. This is only necessary if you did not pick Fanatic as your starter 
specialization

specializations.title: Graveborn
specializations.content:
Graveborn
 is a spec that volunteers for the skeleton war. This is a risky specialization; much like 
 
Purgatorian
, it relies on your own creatures dying, but unlike 
 
Purgatorian
's, yours are supposed to get back up.



Contents




1
 
Game description


2
 
Starter creatures

Strategy






 Game description
[
edit
]


The Graveborn is a once-powerful warlock who was killed for his heresy and has since risen from the dead to wreak havoc on the living. This undead spellcaster is capable of bringing his creatures back from the dead with far greater power than before.


As a Graveborn, you'll bring empowered versions of your creatures back from the dead to wreak havoc on your enemies.

specializations.title: Grovetender
specializations.content:
Grovetender
 is something of a fusion of 
 
Animator
 and 
 
Warden
: the specialization is centered around the 
Herbling
, that little turnip that can turn into a terrifying plant-beast.



Contents

Game description


2

[
edit
]


Grovetenders take great pride in their Herbling companions, strongly preferring to focus the bulk of their efforts on this prized creature over all the others. This peculiar mage knows just the right techniques to bring out her Herbling's full potential as it transforms rapidly in combat to wreak havoc on its foes.


As a Grovetender, you will always have a unique creature in your party called an Herbling. During battle, your creatures' actions will fertilize the Herbling, causing it to rapidly evolve until it turns into an unstoppable force of nature.

specializations.title: Hell Knight
specializations.content:
Hell Knight
 is a class specialised in very strong attacks, and setting enemies on fire in case just hitting things really hard doesn't work (which won't happen often, because 
 
Hell Knights
 have several perks that let them outright ignore several common strategies that shut attackers down). 

Pump that 
Attack
 and kill things in one mighty strike.

2
 
Starter creatures

 Game description
[
edit
]


Careful planning and uptight caution are nothing but a cause to doubt oneself. Hell Knights realize this fact, and instead opt to send their creatures charging headlong into battle, relying on brute strength and reckless attacks to ensure a swift victory.


As a Hell Knight, you'll favor creatures that specialize in attacking. Hell Knights grant their creatures increased critical damage and cause a variety of effects to occur when they attack. Hell Knights are surprisingly adept at spellcasting in a pinch, as well.

 Game description
[
edit
]


 Burn the witch, judge the heretic, and slay the non-believer. Inquisitors make use of healing effects just as much for offense as they do for defense. They enact justice on behalf of the gods, and no one will be spared from holy rapture.


As an Inquisitor, you'll have a solution at your disposal for every problem you encounter. In addition, Inquisitors are able ot use healing effects for offensive purposes - for example, when your creatures heal enemies, they instead cause damage to them.

Some creatures are blessed with protectors that guard them from harm no matter the cost. The Mermaid relies on her Benthic Guardian to shield her other creatures from danger, ensuring that her allies can fight without fear while her guardian bears the brunt of the enemy's fury.


As a Mermaid, you'll focus on healing your creatures and raising their defenses, making them incredibly durable. Your Benthic Guardian serves as the heart of your strategy, acting as both a shield and a conduit for your most powerful effects.

specializations.content:
Mesmerist
 focuses on debuffing their enemies, particularly with 
Sleeping
 and 
Blind
, as well as providing their own creatures with empowered 
Arcane
 and 
Shelled
 buffs.



Contents




1
 
Game description


2
 
Starter creatures


3
 
Perks


4
 
Trivia






 Game description
[
edit
]


A blade can cut the body, but the mind is far more fragile. The Mesmerist specializes in warping reality itself, putting her enemies to sleep, blinding them, and turning their own senses against them until they no longer know who - or what - they're fighting.


As a Mesmerist, you'll manipulate buffs and debuffs to disrupt your enemies while empowering your own creatures. Sleeping and Blind enemies suffer devastating penalties, while your creatures twist spells and status effects into unexpected weapons.

minions
. Necromancer is adept at summoning minions and keeping them from going away, and benefits greatly from even having them around.

Necromancers prefer to overwhelm their foes by summoning minions to aid their creatures in battle. Enemies are often so perplexed by the number of undead monstrosities charging at them from across the battlefield that they're dead long before they realize what even happened.


As a Necromancer, you'll summon minions to aid your creatures in battle. These minions can do all kinds of things to support your creatures - they can cause damage, boost your creatures' stats, debuff enemies, and much more.

Purgatorians blur the line between life and death. These mystical spellcasters are able to call upon the spirits of their slain creatures to continue fighting in the afterlife.


As a Purgatorian, your creatures are able to continue fighting even while they're dead In fact, thanks to your powers, your creatures will be even stronger in the afterlife. Be careful not to let all your creatures die, though, or it's game over!

Strategy
[
edit
]


(cited from gay moth aunt's spec guide)

 
Purgatorian
 is a 
very
 high-risk class, since the class's perks rely on most of your team being dead - and thus your enemies will have fewer targets to kill. 
 
Designated Survivor
 and 
 
Masochism
 can blunt this, you should still prepare your team build very carefully to allow your final creature to stay standing.

While your dead creatures can still act on your behalf and more strongly than they would while alive, and are effectively beyond retaliation, they still act randomly which gives you less ability to focus down priority targets.

There are a handful of existing traits that allow creatures to act while dead that could synergize with this specialization - in addition, revival-oriented creatures such as 
 
Wight
s and 
 
Phoenix
es may be of high value to a 
 
Purgatorian
 looking for perk synergies.

Pyromancers quite literally thrive on watching the world burn. These maniacal magi ignite both friend and foe alike with a brilliant conflagration, causing their enemies to suffer gruesome deaths while empowering their allies with the power of flame.


As a Pyromancer, you'll make heavy use of the Burning debuff to deal damage to enemies over time. In addition, your own creatures will be afflicted with this debuff, but it will heal and empower them rather than dealing damage.

specializations.title: Shadowbringer
specializations.content:
One brings shadow, one brings light

 
Shadowbringer
 is focused on Blight,

which is a cruel debuff that not only prevents enemies from healing, but turns any healing into damage. 
 
Shadowbringer
 perks help you double down on 
Blighted
 damage by force-healing enemies with spells and by giving them buffs like 
Mending
 that becomes damage over time when combined with 
Blighted
, and 
Leeching
 that punishes them for dealing damage.

As a Shadowbringer, you'll utilize the Blighted debuff to damage your foes using numerous healing effects.

even if they are one already
. This specialisation is to 
Defense

The Siegemaster is a stalwart protector who amplifies the defenses of his creatures to keep them strong in battle.


As a Siegemaster, you'll protect your creatures with powerful defensive mechanisms while they reflect damage taken back on their attackers. This specialization makes great use of the Defend command in battle.

Sorcerers are masters of controlling the battlefield. Why worry about offense or defense when your enemies can't move to begin with?


As a Sorcerer, you'll support your creatures by debilitating their enemies from afar. Generally, your enemies won't be able to act for the first few turns in battle as long as you're around.

Chaos, Death, Life, Nature and Sorcery: the five components that gave birth to the land of Rodia. Spellweavers can call upon these elements to imbue their creatures with powerful inscriptions to decimate their foes in battle.


As a Spellweaver, your creatures can cast spells to produce Infusions which can be expended for powerful effects. After your creatures accumulate enough Infusions, they can cast the powerful Colorwave spell to annihilate their enemies.

Toxicologists spend their days tarrying away at all manner of potions and elixirs that might serve their use in battle. These mad scientists care little for the well-being of their own creatures, equipping them with venomous toxins that grant them enhanced combat prowess at the cost of their own lives.


As a Toxicologist, you'll use the Poison debuff to inflict massive damage over time to your foes. You'll also Poison your own creatures to generate beneficial effects when they take damage.

specializations.title: Tribalist
specializations.content:
Tribalist
 sees the synergies of creatures that belong to the same species and quadriples down on them.

[
edit

Strength in numbers. Tribalists prefer to use creatures that belong to the same race or class, and take full advantage of the synergy and camaraderie they offer each other.


As a Tribalist, you'll want to assemble a party of creatures that belong to the same race. This will provide them with significant benefits that are exacerbated by their racial synergies with each other.

Since you are going to be mono-race and (likely) mono-class, traits from the

Six creatures in your team (6)

's Odd One trait (11)


Two from 
Shackler's

Shackler's Mastery
 (13)


If using Diabolic Horde, Three from 
Diabolic Rebel
's Diabolic Revolution (16)

Luck is not merely a matter of chance, but instead a force that can be manipulated to a clever man's will. Tricksters capitalize on this fact, enabling their creatures to re-roll the proverbial dice whenever fate doesn't turn out quite they way they hoped.


As a Trickster, you'll heavily inconvenience enemies by preventing them from taking action. You'll also gain the ability to manipulate the element of luck - for example, if a positive effect has a chance to activate but fails to do so, you can try again.
